title: Hot chip
text:
Hot chip 

There are also several physical constraints that will, for now, impose limits on the speed at which models can improve themselves. The most important is access to compute. Despite efficiency gains, newer models continue to use more computing power to train than their predecessors, forcing progress to occur at the pace of data- centre development. 

Consumer use of AI may also slow down AI-powered research and development, says Helen Toner, interim executive director of CSET and a lead author of its recent report. The limited capacity in AI data centres needs to be carefully split between serving paying customers, training future models and carrying out open-ended R&D. The more demand there is in the first cate gory, the less capacity, in the short term, there is for the other two. 

Then there is the issue of training data. Much recent progress in AI has been in areas where models can teach themselves how to succeed thanks to “verifiable rewards”. A piece of software either runs or it does not; a mathematical proof is correct or it is not. In such cases synthetic data, generated by models purely to train other models, can be checked for accuracy and added to the training data without risking the degeneracy that normally comes with training an AI on its own output. It is trickier to make a model better at creative writing or legal judgment. If the models need to learn from the real world, that could also limit the reach of self-improvement. 

“Closing the loop” may be a step on the road to superintelligence and—depending on your disposition—utopia or doom. But it is not the only step required to produce exponential growth in AI’s capabilities.

quizlet: train an AI on its own output
training an AI on its own output

questions.question: According to the article, what is currently the most important physical constraint on AI self-improvement?
questions.explanation: The article identifies access to compute as the most important limitation.

questions.question: Why might consumer demand for AI services slow future AI development?
options: Data-centre resources must be shared between customers and research
questions.explanation: Serving customers consumes computing resources that could otherwise be used for training and R&D.

questions.question: Why are 'verifiable rewards' important for AI training?
options: They allow AI-generated data to be checked for correctness
They eliminate the need for computers
questions.explanation: Tasks with objectively correct answers make it easier for models to generate reliable training data.

questions.question: What problem can arise when AI is trained on its own outputs?
options: The model may suffer from degeneracy
questions.explanation: The article warns about degeneracy when AI repeatedly learns from AI-generated content.

questions.question: What is the author's overall conclusion about recursive self-improvement?
options: It guarantees superintelligence within a year
It could be important, but it is only one factor behind future AI progress
It will replace all forms of scientific research
questions.explanation: The article concludes that closing the loop may matter greatly, but it is not the only driver of future AI growth.
